carcinogens.description: Meat preserved by salting, curing, fermentation, smoking: hot dogs, ham, sausages, corned beef, beef jerky, canned meat.
carcinogens.mechanism: N-nitroso compounds formed during processing; heme iron; PAHs from smoking.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 1: Carcinogenic. Limit or avoid processed meat consumption.

carcinogens.description: All alcoholic beverages — beer, wine, spirits.
carcinogens.mechanism: Ethanol → acetaldehyde (carcinogenic metabolite). Also increases estrogen.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 1: Carcinogenic. No safe level. Less is better.

carcinogens.description: Naturally occurring mycotoxins in grains, peanuts, tree nuts, corn, dried fruits.
carcinogens.mechanism: Produced by Aspergillus fungi. DNA-damaging. Causes hepatocellular carcinoma.
carcinogens.advice: Controlled by food safety regulations. Avoid moldy grains/nuts.

carcinogens.description: Betel leaf wrapping areca nut and tobacco.
Unprocessed mammalian muscle meat: beef, veal, pork, lamb, mutton, horse, goat.
carcinogens.mechanism: Heme iron, N-nitroso compounds formed during digestion, heterocyclic amines from cooking.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2A: Probably carcinogenic. Limit to ≤500g/week (WCRF recommendation).

carcinogens.description: Formed when starchy foods are cooked at high temperatures (frying, baking, roasting).
carcinogens.mechanism: Formed from asparagine + sugars at >120°C (Maillard reaction byproduct).
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2A: Probably carcinogenic. Reduce frying and over-browning of starchy foods.

carcinogens.description: Sodium/potassium nitrite/nitrate used as preservatives in processed meats.
carcinogens.mechanism: Form N-nitroso compounds (nitrosamines) in the stomach and during high-heat cooking.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2A for conditions leading to nitrosamine formation. Found in cured/processed meats.

carcinogens.description: Any beverage consumed at very high temperatures.
carcinogens.mechanism: Thermal injury to esophageal lining promotes cancer development.
carcinogens.advice: Let hot beverages cool slightly before drinking. Not ingredient-related.

carcinogens.description: Artificial sweetener (E951) used in diet sodas, sugar-free products.
carcinogens.mechanism: IARC classified in July 2023 based on limited evidence in humans.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2B: Possibly carcinogenic. WHO ADI of 40 mg/kg/day still applies.

carcinogens.description: Antioxidant preservative (E320) used in fats, oils, snack foods.
carcinogens.mechanism: Causes forestomach tumors in rodents at high doses.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2B: Possibly carcinogenic. Commonly found in processed foods.

carcinogens.description: Byproduct of caramel color (E150d) manufacturing. Found in cola, soy sauce, dark beers.
carcinogens.mechanism: Lung tumors in rodent studies. Present in Type III and IV caramel colors.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2B: Found in colas and dark-colored beverages/sauces.

carcinogens.description: Formed during heat treatment of food: canned foods, coffee, baby food jars.
carcinogens.mechanism: Formed during thermal processing. Hepatotoxic and possibly carcinogenic.
carcinogens.advice: IARC Group 2B: Minimize consumption of heavily thermally processed canned goods.

carcinogens.description: Whitening agent (E171) used in candies, chewing gum, toothpaste, sauces.
carcinogens.mechanism: Nanoparticle concerns. EFSA concluded not safe → BANNED in EU since 2022.
carcinogens.advice: BANNED in EU. IARC Group 2B. Avoid products containing E171.
